requirements.title: Domain validation
requirements.text: The system shall validate that Domain values are in the range [0, 232] inclusive and return DomainOutOfRange for values outside this range.

requirements.title: Participant trait
requirements.text: The system shall expose a Participant trait with new_publisher, new_subscriber, domain, and close methods per RELAY spec §8.2.

requirements.title: new_publisher rejects empty topic
requirements.text: Participant::new_publisher shall return TopicEmpty when the topic argument is an empty string.

requirements.title: new_subscriber rejects empty topic
requirements.text: Participant::new_subscriber shall return TopicEmpty when the topic argument is an empty string.

requirements.title: Participant close is idempotent
requirements.text: Participant::close shall be idempotent; calling it more than once shall return Ok.

requirements.title: Operations after close return Closed
requirements.text: Calls to new_publisher and new_subscriber after Participant::close shall return Error::Closed.

requirements.title: Publisher trait
requirements.text: The system shall expose a Publisher trait with write, write_ctx, and close methods per RELAY spec §8.2.

requirements.title: write delivers to subscribers
requirements.text: Publisher::write shall deliver the payload to all active subscribers on the same topic.

requirements.title: write_ctx honours context deadline
requirements.text: Publisher::write_ctx shall return Error::Timeout when the context deadline has passed before the write completes.

requirements.title: Publisher close is idempotent
requirements.text: Publisher::close shall be idempotent; write after close shall return Error::Closed.

requirements.title: Subscriber trait
requirements.text: The system shall expose a Subscriber trait with unsubscribe and close methods per RELAY spec §8.2.

requirements.title: SampleReceiver
requirements.text: The system shall expose a SampleReceiver with async recv() and non-blocking try_recv() for consuming samples.

requirements.title: Sample fields
requirements.text: Sample shall carry topic, payload, timestamp, sequence_number, and writer_guid fields per RELAY spec §15.7.2.

requirements.title: Unsubscribe stops delivery
requirements.text: Subscriber::unsubscribe shall stop sample delivery without closing the SampleReceiver channel.

requirements.title: sequence_number is monotonic
requirements.text: Each publisher shall assign monotonically increasing sequence numbers to samples written on a topic.

requirements.title: ReliabilityKind
requirements.text: The system shall define BestEffort and Reliable reliability kinds per RELAY spec §8.2.

requirements.title: DurabilityKind
requirements.text: The system shall define Volatile and TransientLocal durability kinds per RELAY spec §8.2.

requirements.title: DefaultQoS
requirements.text: DEFAULT_QOS shall be BestEffort + Volatile with history depth 1.

requirements.title: ReliableQoS
requirements.text: RELIABLE_QOS shall be Reliable + TransientLocal with history depth 1.

requirements.title: TransientLocal cache
requirements.text: TransientLocal subscribers shall receive the last history_depth samples cached before they joined.

requirements.title: Back-pressure DropNewest
requirements.text: DropNewest policy shall drop the arriving sample when the subscriber channel is full.

requirements.title: Back-pressure DropOldest
requirements.text: DropOldest policy shall drop the oldest buffered sample to make room for the arriving sample.

requirements.title: Mandatory sentinels
requirements.text: The system shall expose Closed, NotConnected, Timeout, and PayloadTooLarge error variants per RELAY spec §5.1.

requirements.title: DDS-specific errors
requirements.text: The system shall expose DDS-specific error variants including DomainOutOfRange, TopicEmpty, QosMismatch, DeadlineMissed, SampleRejected, ResourceLimits, LoanBuffer, and AccessDenied.

requirements.title: Error sentinel mapping
requirements.text: Error::as_relay() shall map each DDS-specific error to the nearest mandatory RELAY sentinel.

requirements.title: Sample to/from Message
requirements.text: Sample::to_message() and Sample::from_message() shall provide lossless round-trip conversion per RELAY spec §15.7.2.

requirements.title: RELAY Node adapter
requirements.text: adapt() shall wrap a Participant as a relay::Node routing send() to per-topic publishers and subscribe() to DDS subscribers.

requirements.title: subscribe requires topic
requirements.text: relay::Node::subscribe() shall return Error::NotConnected when SubscriberOptions has no topic set.

requirements.title: RELAY_SPEC_VERSION
requirements.text: The crate shall export RELAY_SPEC_VERSION = "1.10" per RELAY spec §19.4.

requirements.title: MockParticipant creation
requirements.text: MockParticipant::new shall validate the domain and return an in-process participant requiring no OS or network dependencies.

requirements.title: Mock broadcast
requirements.text: MockParticipant shall deliver samples written by a publisher to all active subscribers on the same topic.

requirements.title: Mock write log
requirements.text: MockParticipant shall record all written payloads accessible via written_payloads() and clearable via reset().

requirements.title: Concurrent safety
requirements.text: All trait implementations shall be safe to use from multiple async tasks concurrently (Send + Sync).

requirements.title: AtomicBool for closed flag
requirements.text: Participant and Publisher closed state shall be tracked with AtomicBool and checked before every operation.

requirements.title: Fault propagation (ISO 26262 ASIL-D)
requirements.text: All critical operations shall detect and propagate faults to the caller via the Error type; no silent failure or panic in safety-relevant code paths.

requirements.title: No unsafe code without justification (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: No unsafe Rust shall be used in any module without a documented safety argument justifying the absence of undefined behavior.

requirements.title: No panic in safety paths (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: No .unwrap() or .expect() shall appear in code reachable from safety-relevant API entry points; all Results shall be propagated.

requirements.title: Diagnostic coverage (ISO 26262 Part 5)
requirements.text: The system shall support diagnostic coverage measurement; every safety function shall be exercised by at least one test carrying a fusa:test annotation.

requirements.title: Sequence number overflow detection (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: Publisher sequence numbers shall be u64 monotonically increasing per-writer; the application shall detect rollover as a safety violation.

requirements.title: Input boundary validation (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: The Domain type shall reject values outside [0, 232] before any participant is created; boundary values 0 and 232 shall each be covered by an explicit test.

requirements.title: Deterministic state machine (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: Publisher and Participant state transitions (open → closed) shall be implemented with AtomicBool compare_exchange to prevent concurrent duplicate-close races.

requirements.title: Graceful allocation failure (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: Memory allocation failures in queue operations shall be handled via the back-pressure policy rather than panic; no unbounded allocation in write paths.

requirements.title: API contract documentation (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: All public APIs shall document their safety pre-conditions, post-conditions, and error returns in Rustdoc.

requirements.title: No busy-wait in safety paths (ISO 26262)
requirements.text: No thread::sleep or spin-loop shall be used in any path reachable during normal operation; blocking waits shall use async/await with tokio.

requirements.title: Testability without live dependency (IEC 61508 SIL-3)
requirements.text: All safety functions shall be testable in isolation using MockParticipant without any OS, network, or external process dependency.

requirements.title: Fail-safe on error (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: The system shall implement fail-safe behavior: on any detected error, return the error and halt further processing for that operation rather than attempting recovery that could mask the fault.

requirements.title: Bidirectional traceability (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: Every requirement in requirements.json shall have at least one fusa:req implementation annotation and at least one fusa:test test annotation, enabling bidirectional traceability.

requirements.title: Test completeness gate (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: CI shall enforce that every requirement ID appearing in requirements.json also appears in at least one fusa:test annotation in the test suite.

requirements.title: Output validity (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: The system shall not deliver samples to closed or unsubscribed channels; all output shall pass through the unsubscribed/closed guard before delivery.

requirements.title: Formal synchronization (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: All shared mutable state shall be protected by formal synchronization primitives (Mutex, AtomicBool); no unprotected shared state shall exist.

requirements.title: Drop counter on back-pressure (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: The system shall maintain visibility into dropped samples; future implementations shall expose drop counters accessible for diagnostic monitoring.

requirements.title: Deterministic safety functions (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: All safety functions shall be deterministic and free of external environmental dependencies; tests shall not rely on wall-clock timing except for explicit timeout tests.

requirements.title: Exhaustive error type (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: The Error enum shall be exhaustively matched in as_relay(); the wildcard arm (_) shall handle only variants with no RELAY sentinel mapping.

requirements.title: Atomic lifecycle enforcement (IEC 61508)
requirements.text: Participant and Publisher lifecycle transitions (open → closed) shall be enforced with AtomicBool::compare_exchange to guarantee at-most-once close semantics.

requirements.title: Bidirectional traceability (DO-178C DAL-A)
requirements.text: Every line of safety-relevant source code shall be traceable to at least one requirement via fusa:req, and every requirement shall be traceable to at least one test via fusa:test.

requirements.title: No dead code in safety modules (DO-178C)
requirements.text: No unreachable or dead code shall exist in participant.rs, error.rs, types.rs, or adapt.rs; CI lint shall fail on dead code warnings.

requirements.title: Decision coverage gate (DO-178C Modified Condition/Decision Coverage)
requirements.text: Every conditional branch in safety-relevant code shall be exercised by the test suite in both the true and false directions.

requirements.title: Documented assumptions (DO-178C)
requirements.text: All non-obvious constraints, assumed pre-conditions, and workarounds shall be documented with inline comments in the source.

requirements.title: Runtime version and conformance export (DO-178C)
requirements.text: The crate shall export RELAY_SPEC_VERSION and a capabilities function returning the protocol, version, and feature set at runtime.

requirements.title: Boundary condition tests (DO-178C)
requirements.text: Input boundary conditions (domain 0, domain 232, empty topic, max-depth queue) shall each have a dedicated test exercising the boundary value exactly.

requirements.title: Golden vector tests (DO-178C)
requirements.text: The Sample-to-Message conversion and back shall be verified by a golden-vector test using a known-good input/output pair with hardcoded expected bytes.

requirements.title: Concurrent path coverage (DO-178C)
requirements.text: Tests shall exercise concurrent publish and subscribe paths to detect data races; multi-subscriber broadcast and sequence number ordering shall be verified.

requirements.title: Topic name validation
requirements.text: The system shall reject topic names that are empty, exceed 256 bytes, or contain null bytes; TopicEmpty shall be returned for all such inputs.

requirements.title: Payload size limit
requirements.text: Publisher::write shall enforce a configurable maximum payload size; PayloadTooLarge shall be returned for payloads exceeding the limit.

requirements.title: Access denied error
requirements.text: The system shall expose an AccessDenied error variant to be returned when topic access is denied by an access control policy.

requirements.title: GUID deserialization validation
requirements.text: The dds.writer_guid field in relay::Message meta shall be validated on deserialization: it must be exactly 32 hex characters encoding 16 bytes.

requirements.title: No internal state in error messages
requirements.text: Error message strings shall not expose memory addresses, internal counters, or implementation details that could assist an attacker in information gathering.

requirements.title: Dependency CVE audit
requirements.text: CI shall run cargo audit on every push and pull request; a RUSTSEC advisory for any direct or transitive dependency shall be treated as a build-blocking failure.

requirements.title: Sequence number replay resistance
requirements.text: Monotonically increasing per-writer sequence numbers shall be included in every sample to support replay detection by the application layer.

requirements.title: Replay guard interface
requirements.text: The sequence_number field in Sample shall be exposed to applications so that replay-detection logic can compare against the last-seen sequence number per writer_guid.

requirements.title: Bounded queue allocation
requirements.text: Subscriber queues shall be bounded by the configured channel_depth; the Block back-pressure policy shall never allocate unbounded memory.

requirements.title: No secrets in message metadata
requirements.text: The relay::Message meta map shall not be used to transmit credentials, cryptographic keys, or any secret material; the spec does not define a confidentiality mechanism for meta.

requirements.title: Payload bounds enforcement
requirements.text: No buffer over-read shall occur during payload serialization or deserialization; all byte-slice operations shall use checked indexing or slice operations.

requirements.title: Bounded task spawning
requirements.text: The RELAY adapter shall not spawn unrestricted tasks; each subscribe() call spawns exactly one forwarding task, which terminates when the subscriber is closed.

requirements.title: Topic name character safety
requirements.text: Topic names shall be validated to contain only printable ASCII characters and forward slashes; null bytes and control characters shall cause TopicEmpty to be returned.

requirements.title: AccessDenied RELAY sentinel mapping
requirements.text: Error::AccessDenied shall map to relay::Error::NotConnected via as_relay() to prevent unauthorized access from appearing as a routing or transport failure.

requirements.title: No silent failures
requirements.text: All error conditions shall be surfaced via the Error return type; no safety- or security-relevant condition shall be silently swallowed.

requirements.title: No unaudited unsafe code
requirements.text: No unsafe block shall appear in any source file without a SAFETY comment documenting the invariant that makes it sound.

requirements.title: Bounded queue capacity
requirements.text: All subscriber queues shall be bounded to at most the configured channel_depth; the implementation shall not grow the queue beyond this limit.

requirements.title: Block policy capacity enforcement
requirements.text: The Block back-pressure policy shall be implemented as async backpressure that waits for space rather than growing the queue without bound.

requirements.title: Subscriber deallocation on close
requirements.text: The SubInner queue memory shall be released when the subscriber is closed; no subscriber allocation shall outlive its close() call.

requirements.title: No reference cycles
requirements.text: No Arc reference cycles shall exist in any data structure; all back-references shall use Weak or be avoided by design.

requirements.title: Bounded string allocation
requirements.text: All String values derived from external sources (topic names, meta values) shall be validated for maximum length before being stored.

requirements.title: O(1) push on hot path
requirements.text: SubInner::push shall complete in O(1) time under DropNewest and DropOldest policies; no iteration over the full queue shall occur during push.

requirements.title: Non-blocking try_recv
requirements.text: SampleReceiver::try_recv shall be non-blocking and return immediately with None when no sample is queued.

requirements.title: Close does not block on I/O
requirements.text: Participant::close in mock mode shall not block on any network I/O or external process; it shall complete synchronously after draining the subscriber list.

requirements.title: Bounded write latency
requirements.text: Publisher::write latency shall be bounded by queue capacity times message size under DropNewest or DropOldest; it shall not block indefinitely.

requirements.title: No dynamic allocation on hot path after init
requirements.text: After participant and publisher initialization, the write and deliver paths shall not perform dynamic memory allocation; all queues are pre-allocated to capacity.

requirements.title: relay-conform CI gate
requirements.text: CI MUST run `relay conform --strict` on the release binary and fail if any §12 schema validation returns FAIL or WARN (§20.1).

requirements.title: rsfusa CI gate
requirements.text: CI MUST run `rsfusa check` and fail if any ERROR-level finding is reported (§20.2).
